query: What is the most recent World Bank / Argus Media monthly price of liquid sulphur Tampa in USD per metric ton for 2026? What were the values month by month January through June 2026?
answer:
## Research Complete

### Key finding: the World Bank no longer publishes a sulphur price
I verified directly (reading both the **January 6, 2026** and **June 2, 2026** World Bank Pink Sheets) that **the World Bank Pink Sheet no longer contains a Sulphur/Tampa liquid sulphur series** — its Fertilizers section lists only DAP, Phosphate rock, Potassium chloride, TSP, and Urea. The WB discontinued the series years ago. So a "World Bank monthly liquid sulphur Tampa" figure for 2026 has to come from the **underlying Argus Media / Green Markets Tampa molten sulphur contract**, which is what the historic WB series tracked.

### The values (a quarterly-settled contract, so months repeat within a quarter)

| Month 2026 | Tampa molten sulphur | ≈ USD/metric ton |
|---|---|---|
| January | $495.69/lt CFR | ~$488 |
| February | $495.69/lt | ~$488 |
| March | $495.69/lt | ~$488 |
| April | $655/lt (record) | ~$645 |
| May | $655/lt | ~$645 |
| June | $655/lt | ~$645 |

- **Q1 2026 (Jan–Mar): $495.69/lt CFR** (Green Markets/Argus), up from Q4 2025's $310/lt.
- **Q2 2026 (Apr–Jun): $655/lt delivered — a record**, confirmed by Argus. The jump was driven by the March 2026 US–Israel–Iran conflict disrupting Middle East sulphur exports through the Strait of Hormuz.

### Important caveats
- 1 long tonne = 1.01605 metric tonnes, so $/lt figures are slightly higher than the $/mt equivalents.
- The exact Q1 figure varies by basis (Argus "delivered" vs Green Markets "CFR"; pre-settlement projection was $475–520/lt). Q2 $655/lt is directly confirmed by Argus.
- International **spot** sulphur (China/India CFR) ran much higher (~$1,200/t per Mosaic's Q1 earnings) than the Tampa delivered-contract price — don't conflate the two.

Full structured findings (data points, sources) have been submitted.

sources.snippet: Middle East sulphur exports delayed after vessels backlog outside Strait of Hormuz following March 2026 conflict.
